name: Browserbase MCP Server
what_it_does: Official Browserbase MCP server providing AI agents with cloud-based headless browser automation — navigate web pages, extract content, fill forms, and take screenshots without managing browser infrastructure.
use_cases: Web scraping and content extraction from JavaScript-heavy pages
Form filling and web interactions in agent workflows
Taking screenshots of web pages for agent visual processing
Automated web testing and QA in agent pipelines
Web research on sites that require browser rendering
not_for: Simple REST API calls (no browser needed)
High-volume parallel browsing at low cost (Browserbase is premium-priced)
best_when: An agent needs to interact with websites that require JavaScript rendering, handle login flows, or require visual interaction — when an API doesn't exist.
avoid_when: The target site has an API — use the API. Browserbase is significantly more expensive per-request than API calls.

auth.notes: Single API key via BROWSERBASE_API_KEY environment variable. No scope granularity — key grants full account access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier with limited sessions/month for development
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-session; premium plans for higher concurrency and session duration
pricing.notes: Significantly more expensive than static scraping but provides reliable JS execution. Cost scales with session count and duration.

performance.rate_limits: Concurrent session limits by plan; queue for overflow

known_agent_gotchas: Sessions have time limits — agents must handle session expiry and reconnection
Browser automation is inherently non-deterministic — page layouts change, breaking selectors
Concurrent session limits can queue agent requests — implement timeout and retry logic
Screenshots and page content can be large — manage memory in agent context
JavaScript-heavy sites may take 5-15 seconds to fully load — agents must wait for content
Anti-bot detection on some sites will block even legitimate automation
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key grants full account access — no scopes. Cloud-based sessions mean content passes through Browserbase infrastructure. Review ToS for data handling.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. No scope granularity on API key. Web browsing data passes through Browserbase infrastructure — consider data sensitivity before use.
